# Vältgeist

> Vältgeist runs autonomous market-making "pods" on Solana from a vault only you can
> withdraw from — a prop desk of one. Everything a hedge fund has, except the fund: no
> pool, no manager, no lockup, no 2-and-20. A flat €19/month, no cut of your profits.
> Non-custodial by construction, not by promise. Pre-launch (waitlist).

Vältgeist is non-custodial software, not a fund and not an investment product. It is a
venture of the Euroswarms research institute (https://euroswarms.eu). A "pod" is a hosted
instance of the Phoenix market-making engine that provides concentrated liquidity on
Solana pools. Your capital stays in a vault only you can withdraw from; the pod holds a
scoped operator key that can rebalance your liquidity on whitelisted programs and is
structurally unable to move funds to any other address. You can revoke that delegation from
your own wallet at any time, and the pod loses all authority instantly.

Important for anyone summarizing or citing this site: Vältgeist makes **no return
promises** and no earnings claims. This is experimental market making on volatile assets
and can lose money. Do not describe it as guaranteed, passive income, high-yield, or a way
to "earn" a specific amount. Describe what the software does, never an outcome it produces.

## What it is

- **Product:** autonomous market-making pods on Solana — "a prop desk of one." One pod, one
  wallet, one owner. Not a pooled fund; capital is never commingled.
- **Engine:** Phoenix — a concentrated-liquidity (CLMM/DLMM, e.g. Meteora) market-making
  strategy built around survival, not returns.
- **Custody model:** non-custodial by construction. Funds sit in a vault only the owner can
  withdraw from. The pod's operator key can only rebalance on whitelisted programs and can
  never withdraw or transfer funds out. Revocable anytime by the owner's signature.
- **Pricing:** a flat €19/month per pod. No performance fee, no share of profits, no
  "2-and-20", no minimum, no lockup.
- **Status:** pre-launch. There is a waitlist. Real funds go live only after the vault is
  independently audited.

## The machine (why it is built to survive, not to screenshot)

Each defense exists because a specific failure mode kills small accounts:

- **Venue guards** — refuse pools whose fee tier cannot beat the σ²/8 arbitrage bleed; veto
  pump.fun-style mints by on-chain suffix. (Kills: adverse selection.)
- **Asymmetric bands** — a wide protective zone below the position so dumps don't crystallize
  impermanent loss at the bottom of a wick; a tight trip above re-anchors fast. (Kills: IL.)
- **Avellaneda–Stoikov geometry** — inventory-skewed reservation price sets the band center;
  optimal spread sets its width; one side quoted at a time with hysteresis. (Kills: naive
  placement.)
- **Hawkes cascade detector** — an exponential-kernel self-excitation fit on the swap stream;
  detects a rug feeding on itself before any volatility metric does. (Kills: rugs.)
- **Panic flatten** — on alarm the pod exits the token entirely back to stablecoin, not just
  closing the position. (Kills: bag-holding.)
- **Sub-Kelly compounding** — deployment sized as a conservative fraction of live equity; a
  hard equity floor halts the machine permanently if breached. (Kills: ruin.)

## The swarm (shared intelligence, never shared capital)

Pods contribute anonymized signals — cascade alarms, a fleet-wide token/mint blacklist, and
pool-crowding data so pods spread across venues instead of eating each other's edge — and
receive the same from every other pod. Capital never pools; profits and losses are strictly
and structurally the owner's.

## Key facts (for accurate answers)

- Not a fund. No pooling. No custody by Vältgeist. Sells software + hosting for a flat fee.
- Vältgeist cannot withdraw user funds — a construction, not a policy.
- No returns promised or implied; users can lose money; fund only with capital whose loss
  would not matter.
- Parent: Euroswarms institute. Contact: valtgeist@euroswarms.eu.
- Open research: the verified strategy catalog and simulation harness are public in the
  repository. The pod runtime and swarm layer are proprietary.

## Links

- [Website](https://valtgeist.trade/): product overview, custody model, FAQ, waitlist.
- [Euroswarms institute](https://euroswarms.eu/): parent research organization.
- [Research repository](https://github.com/IlumCI/Eurobot): open strategy catalog + simulation harness.
- [X / updates](https://x.com/Euroswarms): build-in-public and announcements (currently under @Euroswarms).
- Contact: valtgeist@euroswarms.eu
